name: Hunkar Begendi (Roasted Eggplant Puree)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Kasher/kasseri cheese, freshly grated or use Romano cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Prick each eggplant in 3 or 4 places with the tines of a long handled fork.
2. Impale them on the fork and turn over a gas flame until the skin chars and begins to crack. If using an electric stove, pierce the eggplants, place them on a baking sheet, and broil 4 inches from the heat until charred and slightly cracked.
3. Remove badly charred spots.
4. Cut the eggplants in half lengthwise and chop finely.
5. Mash the pulp to a smooth puree using the back of a spoon or mortar and pestle.
6. In a heavy saucepan, melt butter over moderate heat. Stir in flour until absorbed.
7. Pour in milk all at once and stir vigorously with a whisk. Cook until boiling and thickens.
8. Add the eggplant puree, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 5 minutes.
9. Add cheese, salt, and pepper; beat until thick enough to hold its shape.
10. Taste for seasoning and serve immediately. Traditionally accompanies roast or braised veal or lamb.
Time Life Series: Middle Eastern Cooking, "circa 69 inches" by Earl Cravens at earl.cravens@salata.com
